summary: An exact pivot must inspect all R source rows to distinguish every present product-store price; one hash-aggregation pass matches this linear lower bound.

optimality.argument: Changing the price or store label of any one input row can change one output cell, so an exact query must inspect all R rows in the worst case. Conditional hash aggregation examines each row once and updates one fixed-size product group, giving O(R) logical time.

replacement_checks.evidence: Every source row may supply the only value for one product-store output cell, so skipping any row can produce an incorrect pivot.
The query performs one grouped aggregation pass with three constant-count conditional aggregate slots per product.
Authored fixtures cover sparse and dense products, each individual store boundary, unsorted and interleaved source rows, multiple products, and NULL output cells.
